gpt55_review_summary: GPT-5.5 review found that strict release bundles did not require dependency freshness evidence, allow reasons were only non-empty checks, committed evidence was not reproducibility-tested, and the evidence README had a blank line at EOF; all findings were resolved.
verification: poetry run pytest -q tests/test_dependency_freshness.py tests/test_release_bundle.py tests/test_public_package.py tests/test_project_governance.py::test_v1_requirements_matrix_tracks_goal_evidence_and_remaining_gates tests/test_project_governance.py::test_document_package_readme_lists_public_modules_and_console_scripts -> 105 passed

poetry run python -m document_kv_cache.pr_evidence --validate-directory pr-evidence/dependency-freshness-evidence --output-json pr-evidence/dependency-freshness-evidence/pr-evidence-validation.json -> ok=true
what_changed: Added dependency freshness evidence generation, public Cachet/document facades, and committed current evidence for direct package pins, runtime serving-profile pins, and resolver-held transitive drift.

Added a strict dependency-freshness sidecar validator and wired it into release bundles, strict V1 completeness, the release-bundle CLI, package console-script checks, README guidance, and the V1 requirements matrix.

Hardened runtime and transitive allow reasons so stale serving-profile runtime pins require Databricks, compatibility, or runtime validation context and transitive holds require resolver or dependency-constraint context.
Added focused tests for committed evidence reproducibility, release-bundle acceptance/rejection, weak allow reasons, public package exports, CLI behavior, and governance docs.
why: Dependency freshness should be release-auditable in Cachet without relying on ad hoc notes; strict release bundles must not publish with stale package pins or weakly justified runtime holds.
